videos.title: This New Feature Is A Big Deal For Creatives
videos.description:
This new AI feature from Canva is actually amazing for creatives.

They just launched Magic Layers — and it can take ANY image and automatically break it into editable layers.

So if you have a real or AI generated image, Canva can separate things like the background, objects, people, or individual elements.

Then you can rearrange, resize, or completely recompose the image like it was originally built in layers.

That means you can generate one AI image and then turn it into multiple thumbnails, ads, or designs without starting over.

Honestly, if you make YouTube thumbnails, ads, or social content, this is a feature you’ll want to know about.

#ai #canva #aitools #contentcreation #ainews

Build and Sell AI Websites + CRM to Local Contractors (Zite)
videos.description:
Start building with Zite here: https://zite.com

Learn how to build and sell AI websites for local businesses using Zite. In this tutorial, we create a contractor website with a built-in CRM, lead capture form, and automation workflows.

This step-by-step guide shows how to use AI to build full-stack business apps without coding, including database setup, workflows, and dashboards. Perfect for freelancers, agencies, and anyone looking to start an AI automation business.

Topics covered:
• AI website builder for small businesses
• Build a CRM with AI
• Lead generation websites for contractors
• No-code AI tools for agencies
• Automating client workflows
• Zite tutorial for beginners


Prompt:

Create a complete web app solution for a local contractor business (electrician ) that includes a public website and a built-in CRM system for managing customer requests.

The system should consist of two connected apps sharing the same database.

APP 1 — Public Contractor Website
This is the public-facing website where customers can learn about the contractor and submit service requests.

Include the following pages and sections:
• Home page with hero section, services overview, and call-to-action
• Services page (electrical repair, installations, emergency service, inspections, etc.)
• About page describing the contractor business
• Contact / Request Service page with a form

The Request Service form should collect:
• Customer name
• Phone number
• Email
• Address
• Type of service needed
• Description of the issue
• Preferred appointment time
• Urgency (normal / urgent / emergency)

When the form is submitted:
• A new record should be created in a shared database called Service Requests
• Status should default to New Lead
• Timestamp should be automatically saved
• Contractor should receive an email notification about the new request

APP 2 — Contractor CRM Dashboard
Create a second internal app that acts as a simple CRM dashboard for the contractor.

Access should require login, or access can be restricted to a whitelisted contractor email.

Inside the dashboard, the contractor should be able to:

View all incoming service requests in a table view similar to a spreadsheet.

Each request record should include:
• Customer name
• Phone number
• Email
• Address
• Service type
• Description
• Requested time
• Lead status
• Notes
• Date created

Include the ability to:
• Change status (New Lead, Contacted, Scheduled, Completed, Closed)
• Add notes to each request
• Filter and sort requests
• View request details in a clean UI

DATABASE STRUCTURE
Create a database called Service Requests with the following fields:

• id
• customer_name
• phone
• email
• address
• service_type
• description
• preferred_time
• urgency
• status
• notes
• created_at

WORKFLOW AUTOMATION

Create a workflow that runs when a new form submission occurs:
• Trigger: Form submission on Request Service page
• Action 1: Create record in Service Requests database
• Action 2: Send email notification to contractor
• Action 3: Mark request status as New Lead

EMAIL NOTIFICATION

Send an email to the contractor that includes:
• Customer name
• Phone
• Service requested
• Message/description
• Link to open the request in the CRM dashboard

EDITABLE WEBSITE CONTENT

Because Zite allows visual editing, the contractor should be able to easily update:
• Business hours
• List of services
• Contact information
• Service descriptions

DESIGN STYLE

Create a clean modern contractor website design that is:
• Mobile friendly
• Simple and professional
• Optimized for small local service businesses

The goal is to demonstrate how freelancers or agencies can build and sell AI-powered websites with built-in CRMs for local contractors.

videos.title: What Permission Does Your Product Give People?
videos.description:
The Smartest Businesses Don't Market Products—They Market Permission
*Get your free Product Marketing Kit packed with launch templates, content planners, and messaging frameworks to promote your product faster.* 🔗 https://clickhubspot.com/5a94b6

Graza and Liquid Death aren't selling olive oil or water. They're selling something way more valuable: permission to be a certain type of person without feeling awkward or judged.

THE PERMISSION FRAMEWORK:

GRAZA OLIVE OIL: That squeeze bottle isn't just convenient—it says "Use the good oil. You're not wasting it." The bright green bottle doesn't feel precious or intimidating. It feels easy. Their content shows normal people cooking normal food, not polished chefs. They're not selling olive oil—they're selling permission to feel confident in the kitchen.

LIQUID DEATH: Water that gives people permission to hydrate without looking like a wellness influencer. No fitness models on beaches. No "best version of yourself" messaging. It just fits into the culture you're already part of—whether that's skateboarding, metal shows, or just wanting edgy packaging.

THE INSIGHT: Features matter, but identity is just as important—if not more. People don't just buy what your product does. They buy who it lets them become without feeling like they're trying too hard.

THE QUESTION TO ASK: "What does this give people permission to be?"
Your product isn't just functional—it's cultural. It either makes people feel confident in their identity or makes them feel awkward. There's no in-between.

What permission does your product grant?

#ProductMarketing #BrandStrategy #MarketingPsychology #PermissionMarketing #BrandIdentity #MarketingStrategy

*Download our free WhatsApp Marketing Toolkit with pre-built templates, AI prompts, and a campaign tracking spreadsheet.* 🔗 https://clickhubspot.com/403302

Meta quietly rolled out an update that might be the biggest advertising opportunity in a decade. And almost nobody's using it yet.

THE OPPORTUNITY CYCLE:
Every major ad platform follows the same pattern—starts wide open with cheap ads, early adopters win big, then everyone floods in, CPMs skyrocket, and the advantage disappears forever. Right now, WhatsApp ads are in that early, underpriced phase.

THE SCALE:
WhatsApp has 2 billion daily active users sending 100 billion messages every single day. For the first time, you can reach that audience for a fraction of what you'd pay on other platforms.

HOW IT WORKS:
Ads appear between full-screen Status updates in the Updates tab. When someone taps, they're taken straight into a chat with your business—instantly. This creates personalized experiences no other advertising platform can match.

THE ADVANTAGE:
WhatsApp ads live inside Meta's ecosystem, so you can run them alongside Facebook and Instagram campaigns. If you already know how to run Facebook or Instagram ads, you already have the skillset. You're just applying it to a channel with next to no saturation and underpriced rates.

THE URGENCY: This window never lasts. Every platform eventually gets crowded. Early adopters capture disproportionate value before competition drives up costs.

videos.title: 3 AI Prompts That Reveal What Your Customers Actually Want
videos.description:
*Grab HubSpot's free Market Research Kit packed with plug-and-play templates and AI prompts for smarter campaigns.* 🔗 https://clickhubspot.com/24c047

Before selling anything, you need to know who your customer is, what they've tried, and why existing solutions fail. This used to take weeks. Now you can do it in under an hour.

THE 3-PROMPT MARKET RESEARCH SYSTEM:

PROMPT 1 - EMOTIONAL BLUEPRINT: "List the top desires, pain points, fears, and dreams of [this customer] who wants [this outcome]. Expand on each."

This reveals the actual emotional language your customers use—not what you think they care about. You get their real words, real frustrations, real motivations.

PROMPT 2 - SOLUTION GAPS: "What are the most common solutions this customer uses to solve this problem, and why do they fail?"

This uncovers the real opportunity. The gaps. The frustrations. The exact reason your product can exist and win.

PROMPT 3 - COMPETITIVE ANALYSIS:
Paste a competitor's sales page and ask: "Analyze the logic, emotional appeal, strengths, weaknesses, and where this positioning breaks."

Now you're not guessing why competitors win—you can see it clearly and identify where they're vulnerable.

THE REALITY: These prompts won't replace talking to real customers. But they'll save you weeks of blind research and help you ask better questions faster.

Stop spending weeks on market research when you can get directional insights in an hour.
